title: State of Distressed: Trucano on Distressed Investing’s Evolution
summary: “In the past, I think a lot of times [sponsors] just handed the keys over if they were hopelessly insolvent,” industry authority David Trucano says. “Now [the market has] basically written, through credit agreements, a call to the equity such that they can extract additional value through the capital structure and preserve value for themselves. And as a fiduciary, they’re required to do it.” Bloomberg Intelligence’s Phil Brendel and Noel Hebert sit down with Trucano (formerly of BlackRock, Cente

title: Odd Lots: Albert Edwards Believes Inflation Will Surge (Podcast)
summary: Making a long career as a bear at a sell-side institution is tough. Generally financial markets have done quite well which means forecasting doom and gloom is, usually, only tenable for so long. Which is why we wanted to talk to one of the most successful bears out there. Société Générale has let Albert Edwards out of the bear cage for today’s episode. Edwards knows his reputation as a bear is well deserved: He believes, among other things, double-digit inflation is in the offing. We also talk a

title: Stellantis Deepens China Bet With Dongfeng Jeep Deal
summary: Stellantis NV and Dongfeng Motor Corp. agreed to jointly make Jeep vehicles in China, expanding the automaker’s renewed push to deepen ties with Chinese partners as it revamps its global strategy.

summary: Japan Post Holdings Co. said it’s aiming for group net income of as much as ¥700 billion ($4.4 billion) and return on equity of 7% in the fiscal year ending March 2029, in a new three-year plan released Friday.

summary: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. plans to expand its wealth-management staff in Hong Kong by 30% this year in response to growing clients’ demand for investments and financing.

summary: Toyota Motor Corp. has filed for approval to build a new manufacturing facility in Texas, building on momentum in the US as President Donald Trump pressures carmakers to bolster domestic production.

summary: CSG’s owner says the company’s share price doesn’t reflect its value and expansion plans.

title: Singapore Home Sales Climb to Six-Month High as Boom Persists
summary: Singapore new home sales surged in April to a six-month high as fresh projects attracted buyers, signaling the market remains buoyant despite the Middle East conflict.

title: Sheinbaum Taps Pemex CFO to Run Oil Giant as Debt Woes Persist

title: Oil Heads for Weekly Gain With Iran War Negotiations at Impasse
summary: Oil headed for a weekly gain as the crucial Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed, with efforts to end the war in limbo and disruptions that have upended global markets set to linger.

title: India’s Gold Demand Slows to a Trickle on Tighter Trade Rules
summary: India’s gold imports are slowing to a trickle as banks and bullion traders grapple with new restrictions aimed at shoring up a weak rupee battered by the Middle East war.

title: Stocks Hit as Inflation Angst Sends Yields Higher: Markets Wrap
summary: A broad selloff in bond markets dragged stocks lower, putting a sudden halt to the artificial intelligence-fueled equity rally that has pushed the S&P 500 from one record high to the next.

title: Global Bond Selloff Worsens as Rising Oil Prices Spook Investors
summary: Investors shed government bonds around the world, propelling borrowing costs to multi-year highs from Japan to the US amid intensifying fears that war-driven inflation will force central banks to pursue higher interest rates.

title: RBI Lifts Bond-Trading Target for Primary Dealers by 48%
summary: The Reserve Bank of India raised trading targets for the country’s bond market makers in a bid to boost liquidity, driving a surge in activity in the 10-year benchmark security, according to people familiar with the development.

title: Adani Group Revives Plan to Seek $1 Billion With Dollar Bond
summary: Indian conglomerate Adani Group is reviving plans to seek about $1 billion through a US-listed dollar-denominated bond, according to people familiar with the matter, a move reflecting its intent to return to the US financing market as its legal troubles there ease.

title: HSBC Drags Feet on $4 Billion Private Credit Investment Effort
summary: HSBC Holdings Plc is yet to make material headway in plans to invest as much as $4 billion into its asset manager’s private credit funds, according to a spokesperson.

title: Xi-Trump Meet Gives China Markets Little Reason to Change Course
summary: Chinese stocks halted their rally and the yuan held steady as the high profile summit between Xi Jinping and Donald Trump delivered a predictable script. The absence of substantiative breakthroughs in trade or diplomacy suggests investors are likely to stick with the status quo.

title: Curious Yen Spikes Have Traders Gaming Out Japan ‘Warning Shots’
summary: Every few trading sessions, the yen jolts higher against the dollar, and then it suddenly drops.

title: Hungary’s Euro Dream Is Reshaping East European Bond Markets
summary: Hungary’s ambition to join the euro is changing the hierarchy of eastern European bond markets in a way that hasn’t been seen in years.

title: Trump Departs Beijing After Highly Choreographed Summit
summary: President Trump heads home from Beijing after a highly choreographed summit with Xi Jinping where their friendliness was on full display but yielded few deals or major breakthroughs. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: These Memory Stocks Are Getting Cheaper Despite Scorching Rally
summary: Insatiable demand for memory chips has some of the industry’s top stocks defying the market’s math, becoming cheaper to own even as their share prices soar to nosebleed levels.

title: Dollar Rallies Toward Best Week Since March on Fed Hike View
summary: The dollar is heading for its best week in two months after US data showed price pressures that could push the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates over the next year.

title: Korea’s World-Beating Stock Rally Stumbles as Global Funds Sell
summary: South Korea’s world-beating stock rally came to a sudden halt as the benchmark tumbled on Friday, underscoring the vulnerability of a market dominated by a pair of chipmakers.

title: BOK’s Hawkish New Board Member Flags Prices, Housing Risks
summary: The Bank of Korea’s newest board member said lower growth rates were preferable to a financial shock as he underlined his hawkish credentials in inaugural remarks.

title: Gold, Silver Fall as US Inflation Fears Fuel Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Metals from gold to copper sank in a broad risk-off move, as a war-driven surge in US inflation fueled expectations for higher interest rates.

title: Ghana’s World-Leading Stock Rally Fuels Prospect of Bank IPOs
summary: A world-leading rally on Ghana’s stock market and an improving economy are likely to encourage lenders to plan initial share sales, according to the head of the nation’s bourse.

title: UAE Will Double Oil Export Capacity Bypassing Hormuz by 2027
summary: The United Arab Emirates will double its capacity to export crude oil bypassing the Strait of Hormuz by next year, as it seeks to reduce reliance on the shipping chokepoint.

title: India’s Modi Condemns Attacks on UAE During Abu Dhabi Visit
summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi strongly condemned attacks on the United Arab Emirates during a meeting with the Gulf nation’s president on Friday, underscoring deepening ties between the two countries as tensions remain elevated in the Middle East.

title: ByteDance Stake Anchors $3 Billion Fund in Win for Investor HSG
summary: HSG, the investment firm formerly known as Sequoia Capital China, has closed a $3 billion continuation vehicle anchored by a stake in ByteDance Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter. The move allowed some US investors to exit the highly-scrutinized asset.

title: Gilts Slump as Investors Brace for Burnham Challenge to Starmer
summary: Gilts tumbled after Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham secured a pathway to potentially challenge Keir Starmer for the UK prime minister’s job, threatening a fresh bout of political instability that investors fear could result in more expansive fiscal policy.

title: Peru’s New Food Generation Looks Beyond the Boom
summary: Peru’s culinary boom has earned global acclaim and turned food into a major economic engine, generating $1.8 billion in culinary tourism in 2024 alone. But the success rests on a fragile chain. Antonia Mufarech reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Morgan Stanley Upbeat on South Africa Outlook Despite Oil Shock
summary: Morgan Stanley remains bullish on South Africa’s longer-term reform trajectory even as a global oil shock challenges the economy, fans inflation and may trigger higher interest rates.

title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang Spotted Eating Noodles on Beijing Sidewalk During Trump Visit
summary:
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was spotted eating Beijing’s famous fried bean sauce noodles.

Huang, who is in Beijing as part of Donald Trump’s state visit to China, seemed perfectly content relishing the local staple right on the sidewalk. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why SocGen's Albert Edwards Sees Double-Digit Inflation Coming Back | Odd Lots
summary: Making a long career as a bear at a sell-side institution is tough. Generally financial markets have done quite well which means forecasting doom and gloom is, usually, only tenable for so long. Which is why we wanted to talk to one of the most successful bears out there. Société Générale has let Albert Edwards out of the bear cage for today's episode. Edwards knows his reputation as a bear is well deserved: He believes, among other things, double-digit inflation is in the offing. We also talk about the attention span of readers on the buy-side, what success looks like for a bear, and how a bear avoids getting fired. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: What's Really Going on in Bond Markets?: 3-Minutes MLIV (Video)
summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson, Tom Mackenzie and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: RBC's Calvasina Says US Industrials 'A Bit Overvalued'
summary: Lori Calvasina, head of US equity strategy at RBC, says "everybody loves industrials in the US," but warns of the sector being "a bit" overvalued. She speaks on Bloomberg Television.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China’s Biggest Courier Is Set to Open Gold Vault in Hong Kong
summary: SF Holding Co., China’s biggest express-delivery firm, is set to open a gold vault in Hong Kong to tap demand for storage as the city pushes forward with plans to become a precious-metals hub.
